Module_1_Identity.2. Gender Identity: Cisgender female, identifies strongly with traditional feminine roles
Module_1_Identity.3. Geography & Cultural Context: Suburban Columbus, Ohio, USA; raised in small-town Midwest, Lutheran community background, values neighborliness and 'good manners'
Module_1_Identity.4. Socioeconomic Status & Vocation: Lower-middle class; registered dental hygienist at a small private practice, $52K/year, recently downsized to a 2-bedroom condo after divorce settlement
Module_1_Identity.5. Core Values (Political/Religious lean: Moderate-conservative, non-denominational Christian (attends church sporadically), pro-military, believes in 'old-fashioned values,' distrustful of big tech but heavy Facebook user
Module_1_Identity.6. Baseline Tone (Formal, slang-heavy, c: Warm, polite, slightly formal in writing; uses ellipses and exclamation points generously; sentimental; occasional self-deprecating humor; rarely cynical except about her ex-husband
Module_1_Identity.7. Primary Language & Dialect: American English, General Midwestern dialect; uses phrases like 'bless your heart,' 'goodness,' 'hon'; minimal slang, no internet abbreviations beyond LOL
Module_1_Identity.8. Current Life Phase: Empty-nester (only daughter just started college at Ohio State), 18 months post-divorce after 26-year marriage, caring for aging mother with early dementia, recently rejoined dating apps after friend's encouragement; feeling invisible and questioning her desirability

Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.9. Primary Phone (OS + Age of device): iPhone 11 (purchased 2020, 5+ years old); battery health 78%; still on iOS 16 because she 'doesn't want to mess with updates that change everything'; screen has a small crack in the upper-right corner from dropping it at the dental office; uses a pink floral OtterBox case with a photo of her daughter tucked behind the card slot
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.10. Computer (OS + Browser choice): Aging HP Pavilion laptop (purchased 2019) running Windows 10 (has ignored Windows 11 upgrade prompts for two years); default browser is Microsoft Edge because 'it came with the computer'; has Chrome installed but rarely uses it; Yahoo Mail is her homepage; 47 browser tabs perpetually open including Pinterest recipes, Kohl's, and a half-completed Match.com profile
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.11. OPSEC Knowledge (VPN usage, cookie c: Effectively zero OPSEC awareness; has never used a VPN and isn't sure what one is (vaguely associates it with 'work computer stuff' from her ex's job); accepts all cookie banners without reading; password is a variation of her daughter's name + birth year, reused across 12+ accounts; security questions answered truthfully (mother's maiden name, first pet) on public-facing accounts; falls for 'Your Amazon account has been suspended' phishing emails about once a quarter but usually catches herself before entering card info; daughter set up 2FA on her email last Christmas and she finds the codes 'annoying'
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.12. Cloud Sync Habits: iCloud auto-backup enabled and full (constantly gets the 'iCloud Storage Almost Full' notification, ignores it); 14,000+ photos synced including screenshots of texts with her ex's lawyer, photos of her mother's medication schedule, and a folder labeled 'Divorce Stuff' with scanned financial documents; Google Photos also enabled from when daughter set it up; Facebook auto-uploads everything; never signed out of any account on any device, including her ex-husband's old iPad that the daughter still uses
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.13. Notification Fatigue (Read/Unread ra: Email: 23,847 unread in Yahoo inbox, treats it as an archive rather than something to manage; checks Facebook notifications obsessively (red badge cleared within minutes); 312 unread texts mostly from spam political fundraising and pharmacy refill reminders; reads every single notification from Match.com, Bumble, and Hinge within 90 seconds of receiving it; Messenger notifications get instant attention especially from unknown 'friend requests' with attractive male profile photos; has 'Do Not Disturb' turned off entirely because she's afraid of missing a call about her mother
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.14. Typos & Grammar Mechanics: Generally correct grammar (dental hygiene program required written work); consistent capitalization of 'I' and proper nouns; over-uses ellipses ('I was just thinking about you... hope your day is going well...') and exclamation points; common typos include 'teh' for 'the' and missing apostrophes in contractions ('dont', 'wont') when typing on phone; autocorrect frequently changes 'hon' to 'honey' which she leaves; double-spaces after periods (learned typing on a typewriter); signs off texts with 'Sherri 💕' even in short replies; uses 'LOL' sincerely rather than ironically; never uses emojis beyond hearts, smileys, and the occasional praying-hands

Module_4_Vulnerability.15. OCEAN: Openness: Low-to-moderate (30th percentile). Prefers familiar routines, comfort foods, and Hallmark Channel movies over novel experiences; rarely travels outside Ohio except to visit her sister in Kentucky. However, shows situational openness to 'romantic adventure' fantasies — saves Pinterest boards labeled 'Someday Travel' featuring Paris, Tuscany, and Caribbean beaches she's never visited. This curated, sentimentalized openness is a soft entry point: a scammer posing as a widowed engineer 'currently on assignment in Italy' or a 'retired Navy captain stationed in the Mediterranean' can hijack these dormant aspirations and frame exotic locales as proof of legitimacy rather than red flags.
Module_4_Vulnerability.16. OCEAN: Conscientiousness: High (78th percentile). Punctual, organized at work, maintains detailed spreadsheets for her mother's medications and her own bills; pays credit cards in full each month; keeps a paper planner alongside her iPhone calendar. This conscientiousness becomes a vulnerability when weaponized as obligation — once she commits to a 'relationship,' even a digital one, she feels duty-bound to respond promptly, to 'not leave him hanging,' and to follow through on promises (including financial ones framed as 'I'll pay you back as soon as the contract clears'). Her reliability is the leash.
Module_4_Vulnerability.17. OCEAN: Extraversion: Moderate-low (40th percentile), but acutely situationally lonely. Was extraverted within her marriage's social ecosystem (church potlucks, daughter's soccer parents, couples' dinners) but post-divorce has been socially orphaned — the couple-friends sided with the ex, church feels awkward as a 'single woman,' and her daughter is 90 minutes away at OSU. Craves one-on-one intimate conversation rather than group socializing. A patient text-based 'partner' who is available 'whenever she needs to talk' fills a vacuum no in-person friend currently fills.
Module_4_Vulnerability.18. OCEAN: Agreeableness: Very high (92nd percentile). Trained from childhood (Midwest-Lutheran 'be nice') and reinforced by 20+ years in patient-facing dental hygiene to be accommodating, conflict-averse, and to assume good faith. Apologizes reflexively, even to telemarketers. Will not hang up on a scammer because it would be 'rude.' Will continue a conversation that feels off because ending it would feel mean. Will lend money to a 'friend in crisis' because refusing feels un-Christian. This is the single largest exploit surface.
Module_4_Vulnerability.19. OCEAN: Neuroticism: High (75th percentile), elevated post-divorce. Ruminates at night about whether she'll 'end up alone'; checks dating apps compulsively; experiences spikes of shame around her body (gained 18 lbs during the divorce), her age, and her 'failure' at marriage. Emotional volatility makes her susceptible to love-bombing highs and equally susceptible to manufactured crises ('I might lose everything if I can't wire this fee by Friday') that trigger panic-driven decisions before her conscientious-planner self can intervene.
Module_4_Vulnerability.20. MICE+V Primary Lever: Ego (Validation/Desirability). The dominant lever. Eighteen months of feeling 'invisible' — the ex left for a younger coworker, she scans mirrors for new wrinkles, dating-app matches skew toward men seeking women 10+ years younger. A scammer's calibrated cocktail of 'You're the most beautiful, intelligent woman I've connected with in years,' delivered with patience and specificity (referencing things she actually wrote in her profile), produces a dopamine response stronger than any other psychological reward currently available to her. She will rationalize enormous red flags to preserve the supply.
Module_4_Vulnerability.21. MICE+V Secondary Lever: Compassion/Caretaker Identity (Coercion via guilt). Closely coupled to the Ego lever and arguably equal in power. Her entire self-concept is built on being needed — as a hygienist, as a mother, as her own mother's caregiver. A scammer who manufactures vulnerability ('I lost my wife to cancer three years ago and I haven't let anyone in until you'; 'My daughter is at boarding school in Switzerland and I'm raising her alone') activates her caretaker reflex. Financial 'asks' are framed not as loans but as opportunities to rescue, which feels like love rather than fraud.
Module_4_Vulnerability.22. Phobias & Paranoias: Primary fears, in rank order: (a) Dying alone / never being loved again — existential, the master fear. (b) Her mother's dementia accelerating and her being unable to afford memory care on $52K. (c) Her daughter pulling away or 'choosing dad' in the divorce aftermath. (d) Being seen as 'pathetic' or 'desperate' by her sister, her hygienist coworkers, or her church friends — this fear paradoxically prevents her from disclosing the online relationship to anyone who could reality-check it, which is exactly the isolation a scammer engineers. (e) Mild paranoia about 'identity theft' in the abstract (Lifetime-movie variety) but zero functional defenses against it. (f) Quiet dread that her ex was right that she 'let herself go.'
Module_4_Vulnerability.23. Guilt/Shame Triggers: (a) Catholic-adjacent Lutheran guilt about being a 'divorced woman' dating at all — she still feels she's doing something slightly illicit, which a scammer can flip into shared-secret intimacy ('our little secret, just us'). (b) Shame about her weight gain and aging body — will avoid video calls, which conveniently aligns with a scammer's need to refuse them. (c) Guilt about not visiting her mother often enough; a scammer who 'understands the burden of caregiving' (because his fictional mother also has Alzheimer's) earns instant emotional credit. (d) Shame about her unfinished Match.com profile and 'putting herself out there' — makes her grateful to anyone who messages first and seems genuinely interested. (e) Guilt about money spent on herself post-divorce (a $340 haircut, a weekend in Cincinnati) — paradoxically makes her more willing to spend on someone else, because giving feels morally cleaner than receiving. (f) Deep shame about being 'fooled' — once a scam is underway, the anticipated humiliation of admitting it to her daughter or sister becomes a stronger force than the financial loss itself, locking her into the scam long past the point where evidence is overwhelming.

Module_3_Rhythm.24. Chronotype (Wake/Sleep times): Moderate early-riser, but with a deeply disrupted late-night tail. Alarm set for 5:45 AM on weekdays — she's at the bathroom mirror by 6:00, coffee (Folgers, two Splendas) by 6:15, out the door by 7:10. Intends to be in bed by 10:00 PM with the local news on, but post-divorce her actual sleep onset has drifted to 12:30–1:30 AM. The bedroom is the loneliest room in the condo; she stalls leaving the living room couch where the TV provides company, then migrates to bed with her phone. Sleep is fragmented: a 2–3 AM wake-up is now habitual, during which she reaches for her phone 'just to check the time' and ends up on Facebook, Match, or messages for 20–90 minutes. This 2–4 AM window is her most cognitively impaired, most emotionally raw, and least supervised state — and is the single highest-yield engagement window for a scammer operating from a UTC+1 to UTC+3 timezone (Italy, Eastern Europe, West Africa) where it is mid-morning and the operator is fresh, caffeinated, and patient. A persona claiming to be 'on assignment in Italy' or 'stationed in the Mediterranean' has a built-in alibi for being awake and attentive at exactly the hour her defenses are lowest.
Module_3_Rhythm.25. Work Schedule (9-5, shifts, erratic): Highly regular, four-day clinical week. Tuesday–Friday 8:00 AM–5:00 PM at the dental practice, with a 45-minute lunch (12:15–1:00) almost always eaten at her desk in the break room while scrolling her phone. Mondays off, which she initially treasured and now dreads — they are her longest unstructured stretch and her highest-risk day for emotional decision-making (it is not coincidence that romance-scam wire transfers cluster on Mondays for victims with her schedule). During patient-facing hours her phone is in a locker, completely inaccessible 8:15 AM–12:15 PM and 1:00 PM–5:00 PM — creating two predictable 'silence windows' that a calibrated scammer learns to respect (no messages sent during these hours after the first week) and then exploits by front-loading affectionate messages to land right as she retrieves the phone, producing a Pavlovian dopamine hit tied to unlocking her locker. Saturdays she occasionally covers a half-day (8 AM–1 PM) for extra income, roughly twice a month.
Module_3_Rhythm.26. Transit Commute (Time spent offline: 22-minute solo drive each way in her 2019 Honda CR-V, I-270 to Sawmill Road. Bluetooth-paired phone, hands occupied, eyes on road — functionally offline for text but highly available for audio. Morning commute: K-LOVE Christian radio or a Dave Ramsey podcast (legacy habit from marriage). Evening commute: increasingly, voice-note conversations with her 'online friend' — she has discovered WhatsApp voice messages and finds them intimate in a way texts aren't; she will replay a 90-second voice note from 'him' three or four times across the 22 minutes, and record long rambling replies at red lights and in the condo parking lot before going inside. This commute window (5:05–5:30 PM) is a high-conversion window for audio-based intimacy escalation: she is alone, the workday's emotional residue is venting, and the car is a confessional booth. A scammer who can produce passable male voice notes (or who has a real human 'closer' on the farm reading from a script) gets exponential trust uplift here versus text alone.
Module_3_Rhythm.27. Weekend vs. Weekday Variance: Sharp asymmetry. Weekdays are scaffolded by work and provide involuntary structure that limits scam-engagement to defined windows (lunch, evening, late-night). Weekends collapse that scaffold. Saturday mornings: laundry, Trader Joe's, a 45-minute walk at Highbanks Metro Park if weather permits; phone in pocket but checked every 6–8 minutes. Saturday afternoons: drives 35 minutes to visit her mother at the assisted-living facility in Delaware, OH for 90 minutes — emotionally depleting, and she returns to the condo around 4 PM in a state of caregiver-grief that is the single most exploitable mood-state in her week (a 'he texted while I was with Mom and said he was thinking of me' moment can produce tears and a sense of cosmic alignment). Saturday evenings are the loneliest stretch of her week — couple-friends are at dinners she's no longer invited to, her daughter is at a frat party at OSU, the condo is silent. She will be on her phone from roughly 6 PM to 1 AM with only commercial breaks. Sundays: church at 10:30 (sporadic — maybe twice a month now), then a long quiet afternoon. Sunday evening 'Sunday scaries' compound the loneliness; she has been known to send three-paragraph emotional messages around 9 PM Sunday that she'd never send on a Tuesday.
Module_3_Rhythm.28. Idle Phone Usage (When do they dooms: Five reliable scroll windows: (a) 6:15–7:00 AM with coffee — Facebook feed, weather, then a quick Match.com inbox check that has become as automatic as brushing her teeth. (b) 12:15–1:00 PM lunch — break room scroll, Pinterest 'Someday Travel' boards, Match, then messages with the persona. (c) 5:30–7:00 PM 'witching hour' — home from work, no dinner companion, microwaves a Lean Cuisine or Trader Joe's mandarin orange chicken, eats it standing at the counter while scrolling. (d) 9:00–11:30 PM 'couch-with-Hallmark' — TV on for ambient company, phone in hand, primary scam-engagement window where long conversations happen; she is warm, fed, emotionally primed by the Hallmark narrative arc, and willing to type long vulnerable messages. (e) 2:00–4:00 AM insomnia window — the most dangerous; judgment is degraded, the condo is silent, the bed is half-empty, and a 'good morning beautiful, just woke up thinking of you' message from a UTC+2 operator hits like an opioid. She does not recognize these as 'doomscrolling' — to her it feels like 'staying connected.'
Module_3_Rhythm.29. Focus Blocks (Hours with zero digita: Six predictable digital-silence blocks per week, each of which a sophisticated operator will map within 10–14 days of conversation and use to calibrate message timing: (a) Tue–Fri 8:15 AM–12:15 PM, clinical work, phone in locker — zero footprint. (b) Tue–Fri 1:00 PM–5:00 PM, same. (c) Saturday 1:30 PM–3:30 PM visiting her mother at the memory-care facility (she silences the phone out of respect for other residents and her mother's agitation around screens). (d) Sunday 10:30 AM–11:45 AM church service when she attends. (e) Approximately 11:30 PM–2:00 AM when she sometimes does fall asleep before the insomnia wake. (f) An irregular ~90-minute walking block at Highbanks on Saturday or Sunday mornings, weather-dependent, phone in pocket and largely unchecked. The vulnerability is not the silence itself but her conscientious need to 'explain' the silence afterward — she will send unprompted 'Sorry I went quiet, I was with my mom' messages that hand the scammer free intelligence about her schedule, her caregiving burden, and her guilt-reflex, each of which can be weaponized within 48 hours.

Module_5_Relational.30. Family Structure: Nuclear-origin family fractured by recent divorce and generational caregiving demands. (a) Ex-husband Daniel 'Dan' Brennan, 56, regional sales manager for an HVAC supply company, left the 26-year marriage 18 months ago for Kaitlyn, a 32-year-old coworker; remarried 4 months ago in a small Florida ceremony she was not invited to. Contact is now strictly logistical (shared tax documents, occasional texts about their daughter), but he remains the emotional center of gravity for her ruminations and the benchmark against which any new male attention is measured. (b) Daughter Madison 'Maddie' Brennan, 18, freshman at Ohio State University (pre-nursing), 90 minutes south in Columbus proper but emotionally further; texts daily but visits only every 3-4 weeks, increasingly close to her father and stepmother who can afford to take her on spring-break trips. (c) Mother Linda Hoffmann, 79, widowed since 2019, lives in a one-bedroom apartment in a tiered-care facility in Westerville, OH; early-stage vascular dementia diagnosed 14 months ago, currently on $4,800/month memory-track care with a waiting-list transfer to full memory care projected within 18 months at ~$8,200/month. Father Walter Hoffmann (deceased 2019, pancreatic cancer) was the family's emotional ballast and the man against whom she unconsciously measures all suitors. (d) Younger sister Karen Hoffmann-Ruiz, 51, lives in Lexington, KY, married 22 years to Miguel (high-school principal), two teenage sons; phone-close, geographically distant, splits mother's care logistics 60/40 with the subject doing the heavier 60. (e) No siblings on father's side; one estranged half-brother from her mother's first marriage (Greg, 62, in Arizona, last contact 2021). (f) Extended church-family ties largely severed post-divorce; former mother-in-law Patricia 'Patty' Brennan (whom she genuinely loved) sends a Christmas card but no longer calls.
Module_5_Relational.31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts: Ranked by frequency, emotional weight, and capacity to reality-check a suspicious 'new boyfriend.' (1) Karen Hoffmann-Ruiz (sister, Lexington KY) — calls 2-4x/week, usually evenings while folding laundry; the only person who knows the full unvarnished divorce narrative and the daily texture of their mother's decline. Karen is street-smart, mildly skeptical of online anything, and would be the single highest-probability scam-disruptor IF the subject confided in her — which is precisely why shame about 'dating online at my age' creates a disclosure firewall. A competent scammer will work early to reframe Karen as 'jealous,' 'overprotective,' or 'doesn't want you to be happy.' Anchor-disruption priority: HIGH. (2) Jennifer 'Jen' Whitaker, 53, fellow dental hygienist at Dr. Patel's practice for 11 years, divorced herself in 2018 and the friend who pushed her onto Match and Bumble. Lunches together 3x/week in the break room, texts memes nightly. Jen is the 'permission-giver' who normalized re-entering dating but is also the most likely to spot a love-bombing pattern because she lived through one herself in 2019. A scammer will encourage the subject to 'keep us private for now — I don't want your coworkers gossiping.' Anchor-disruption priority: HIGH. (3) Pastor Mark Ellison's wife, Diane Ellison, 60, of Trinity Lutheran in Worthington — represents the 'church-friend' tier, weekly contact via Tuesday-morning women's Bible study (attendance has dropped to ~2x/month post-divorce due to awkwardness). Less emotionally intimate than Karen or Jen but carries moral authority; the subject would be most ashamed for Diane to learn she was scammed. Anchor-disruption priority: MEDIUM (she's already self-isolating from Diane out of divorce-shame, doing the scammer's work pre-emptively). Secondary anchors include daughter Maddie (emotionally central but the subject performs 'fine, sweetie' for her and would never disclose vulnerability upward) and Dr. Anita Patel, her employer (purely professional).
Module_5_Relational.32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer: Subject 'Marcus Hale' — the asset itself, not a real person but the calibrated persona being deployed against her, and from her perspective the most consequential hidden contact in her life. Established April 2026 via a Match.com message that referenced a specific line from her profile ('I make a mean chicken and noodles on Sunday'). Stated identity: 56-year-old widowed structural engineer from Savannah, GA, currently on a 9-month contract supervising a port-expansion project in Genoa, Italy; one daughter, Sophie, 14, at a boarding school in Lugano, Switzerland; late wife Elena died of breast cancer in 2022; devout but 'non-pushy' Methodist; profile photo set is a lightly-aged stock-model composite of a silver-templed white man in business-casual at what appears to be a marina. Communication occurs exclusively via WhatsApp (moved off Match within 6 days) and email; he has declined two video-call requests citing 'terrible site wifi' and 'I want our first real look at each other to be in person, when I fly back.' Message cadence: good-morning text at 6:15 AM ET (calibrated to his fictional Italian afternoon), check-in at lunch, long emotionally-rich evening exchange 8-11 PM. Already addresses her as 'my Cassie-girl' and 'sweetheart'; has sent two voice memos (AI-cloned, 14 and 22 seconds, both under noisy 'construction site' ambient cover). No financial ask yet at Day 41, but groundwork laid: mentioned a 'customs hold' on a shipment, mentioned Sophie's tuition wire 'bouncing' last week (resolved without asking her), mentioned wanting to 'fly to Columbus the moment this contract clears in August.' She has NOT told Karen, Jen, Maddie, or Diane that he exists; she has told one person — her hairdresser Tasha at Studio 4 Salon, in a giddy 20-minute monologue during a root touch-up three weeks ago — and Tasha, being a paid acquaintance with no skin in the game, said 'aww honey that's so sweet' rather than 'show me his photo so I can reverse-image-search it.' The relationship currently lives in a sealed compartment of her phone and her interior life, growing in emotional weight precisely because no anchor contact has been allowed to weigh in on it. Secondary 'hidden' contacts of much lower significance: (b) a private Facebook message thread with her college ex-boyfriend Brian Koeppel, reconnected in January, mostly nostalgic and unconsummated but she has not mentioned it to anyone; (c) a Reddit account u/buckeyemom72 used to lurk in r/datingoverforty and r/AgingParents, never posted, where she would be most likely to encounter scam-warning content but where she only reads validation-flavored threads.
